brand.name: NBCOS — National Board for Certification in the Orthopedic Specialties

summary: NBCOS is a not-for-profit credentialing body that administers board-recognized specialty certification exams (OPE-C, OPE-SC, OPE-Radiography, ROT) and a 150+ course continuing-education library for orthopedic allied health professionals, bundled into a $179/year Full Membership. Its core audience is Certified Athletic Trainers who need a documented orthopedic specialty credential to compete for clinical roles across orthopedic offices, hospitals, and urgent care centers. Revenue is recurring annual membership, sustained by a two-year CEU reporting cycle and credential-stacking expansion toward the OPE-CS Clinical Specialist designation.

campaigns.name: Welcome & Onboarding — Signup to Exam Pass
campaigns.objective: Drive new members from signup to their first real value: passing the OPE-C exam and earning the board credential.
campaigns.trigger: New Full Membership created / portal account activated. Send within seconds of signup.
campaigns.segment: New members; branch by signup credential status (ATC candidate vs. program director/educator).
campaigns.rationale: NBCOS's aha moment is passing the OPE-C exam and downloading the credential — but study is self-directed, so without an onboarding sequence new members stall before sitting the exam and never reach the value they paid for. This is the highest-engagement, fastest-to-show-a-win flow.

emails.purpose: Warm welcome, confirm portal access is live, and point to the single first step that starts the path to the OPE-C credential.
emails.subject: Welcome to NBCOS — let's get you OPE-C® certified
emails.preview: Your member portal is open. Here's the fastest first step toward the credential.
keyPoints: Confirm membership is active: exam registration, the OPE Candidate Guide, and 150+ Category A CEUs are all unlocked now
One clear first action — open the Candidate Guide and run the sample exam questions to gauge readiness
Set the goal plainly: pass the OPE-C exam (125 questions, 70% to pass) and put the board credential on your résumé and LinkedIn
Real human reply-to for exam or study questions; preview the short onboarding emails to come

campaigns.name: Free Resource → Full Membership Conversion
campaigns.objective: Convert free Candidate Guide and sample-question downloaders into paid $179/year Full Members.
campaigns.trigger: Downloaded the free OPE Candidate Guide or sample exam questions; has not yet joined.
campaigns.segment: Non-member free-resource users; branch by credential status (practicing ATC vs. early-career/student).
campaigns.rationale: NBCOS has no paid trial — the free Candidate Guide and sample questions are the only on-ramp, so the free-resource-to-membership sequence IS the core revenue play. The hero send must defuse the #1 objection (is the OPE-C actually recognized by employers?) with concrete proof before asking for the join.

emails.purpose: Answer the make-or-break objection — employer recognition and worth — then make the self-serve join the obvious next step.
emails.subject: Is the OPE-C® worth it? What orthopedic employers actually look for
emails.preview: The specialty credential that sets ATs apart across 9,000+ orthopedic offices.
keyPoints: Name the objection directly: general AT certification doesn't document casting, imaging, surgical support, or orthopedic diagnosis — the OPE-C does
Ground recognition in scale: orthopedic offices, hospitals, and urgent care centers increasingly want documented specialty competency
$179/year unlocks all exam access plus 150+ Category A CEUs — the credential and recertification path in one membership
Low-friction close: self-serve JOIN NOW, immediate portal and exam access, no sales call required

campaigns.name: CEU Deadline Reminders — Protect the Credential
campaigns.objective: Get members to log required Category A CEUs before the December 31 deadline so their credential stays active.
campaigns.trigger: Approaching December 31 CEU reporting deadline; member is below the required hours for their reporting year.
campaigns.segment: Active members with incomplete CEU hours; branch by gap size (slightly short vs. far behind).
campaigns.rationale: The hard December 31 CEU deadline is NBCOS's appointment-reminder analog — missing it means credential lapse and a lost renewal. Reminders are the highest-ROI retention move (≈35% fewer missed deadlines), and the 150+ CEU library is already included, so completion is friction-free once they act.

emails.purpose: Surface exactly how many hours remain and route the member straight into the included CEU library to finish before the deadline.
emails.subject: Your CEUs are due Dec 31 — don't let your OPE-C® lapse
emails.preview: Log your Category A hours from the portal. The courses are already included.
keyPoints: State the requirement clearly: 24 hours in year one, 12 in year two, audited by April 1 — and the stakes if hours aren't logged
Remove the cost objection: 150+ Category A CEUs are already part of your membership, no extra purchase needed
Make it concrete and quick — start, complete, and self-report hours directly in the member portal
Reassurance + human help: link to support if you're unsure which hours you still owe

campaigns.name: Renewal & Expansion — Renew + Stack Toward OPE-CS
campaigns.objective: Protect recurring annual membership revenue and grow accounts by nudging credential-stacking toward the OPE-CS Clinical Specialist designation.
campaigns.trigger: Renewal date − 45 days; or member has passed one or two of the three specialty exams (OPE-CS upsell readiness).
campaigns.segment: Active members approaching renewal; branch by health (high engagement / exams passed = expansion, low engagement = save/re-onboard).
campaigns.rationale: Membership is annual recurring revenue, and the natural expansion path — passing all three exams to earn OPE-CS — is a built-in upsell that NBCOS currently leaves to chance. A value-recap renewal that frames the next credential is how you keep the renewal and grow account value at the same time.

emails.purpose: Recap the value earned this year, prompt an early renewal, and frame the OPE-CS as the obvious next step for members who've already passed an exam.
emails.subject: Renew your NBCOS membership — and stack toward OPE-CS
emails.preview: Keep your credential active and turn it into the Clinical Specialist designation.
keyPoints: Quantify their year: credential active, CEU hours logged, exams passed — the proof renewal is worth it
Renewing keeps exam access and the full 150+ CEU library live, so recertification stays effortless
Expansion path: pass all three specialty exams (OPE-SC, OPE-Radiography) to earn the combined OPE-CS Clinical Specialist credential
Low-friction self-serve renewal with a human contact for educators/program directors weighing the institutional tier

campaigns.name: Win-Back — Reinstate a Lapsed Credential
campaigns.objective: Reactivate members who didn't renew or let their credential lapse, and clean the list to protect deliverability.
campaigns.trigger: Membership lapsed / renewal missed; or credential lapsed due to incomplete CEUs.
campaigns.segment: Lapsed members; branch by reason (didn't renew vs. missed CEU deadline) and by whether the OPE-C exam was ever passed.
campaigns.rationale: Lapsed members are a flagged gap with no current reactivation path, and an earned OPE-C credential is a sunk investment they're motivated to protect — making win-back both quick to ship and high-yield (typically recovers 5–15% of inactive contacts).

emails.purpose: Remind the lapsed member what an inactive credential costs them and make reinstatement a one-click, low-friction return.
emails.subject: Your OPE-C® lapsed — but it's not too late to reinstate
emails.preview: Reactivate your credential and stay competitive for orthopedic roles.
keyPoints: Name what lapsing means: the credential you earned no longer reads as current to orthopedic employers
Reconnect to the original motivation — documented specialty competency that differentiates you in clinical hiring
Make return frictionless: reinstate membership and immediately regain exam access and the 150+ CEU library to catch up on hours
Final-step respect: if NBCOS is no longer relevant, an easy way to update preferences or unsubscribe
